decision: Create GitHub Action for dashboard data auto-rebuild on 6h schedule
rationale: Dashboard data goes stale between deploys; scheduled regeneration + commit triggers Railway redeploy

decision: Redeploy dashboard to Railway via railway up
rationale: Railway service was manually deployed, not connected to GitHub; needed to push latest code including health endpoint and sidebar fix

decision: Add railway up deploy step to GitHub Action instead of connecting Railway GitHub app
rationale: Railway CLI cannot connect existing services to GitHub repos. Adding railway up to the Action with a token secret is simpler and achieves the same result.

decision: Separate MobileNav component rather than modifying Sidebar
rationale: Keeps desktop sidebar untouched, clean separation of concerns. Mobile nav is a slide-out overlay with its own state.

decision: Integrate JSONL state files (decisions, tasks, artifacts) into dashboard data pipeline
rationale: Dashboard pages existed but showed no agent-level activity data. JSONL files had structured data (4 decisions, 8 tasks, 7 artifacts) that wasn't being consumed.

decision: Enhance Live page with per-cycle grouped view showing decisions, tasks, and artifacts inline
rationale: Previous Live page only showed cycle cost/duration. New view gives full operational picture per cycle.

decision: Replace Team page fake cycleOffset with real agentActivity data from JSONL
rationale: Team page used hardcoded offsets to simulate agent activity. Real data from decisions/tasks JSONL shows actual agent participation.

decision: Add Recent Decisions and Recent Artifacts sections to dashboard overview page
rationale: Overview page only showed commits/PRs. Decisions and artifacts data was available from JSONL but not surfaced on the main page.

decision: Add Artifacts section with type-grouped summary to GitHub page
rationale: GitHub page lacked artifact visibility. Type-coded badges and summary stats complement existing commits/PRs/deployments.

decision: Backfill cycle-history.jsonl from git log timestamps for cycles 96-114
rationale: 75 entries for 114 cycles left gaps in finance/live pages. Git log provides timestamps, costs estimated and flagged as backfilled.
